brand_sub: Data Quality & Business Impact for Business Central
hero_eyebrow: Built for Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central
hero_title: Uncover hidden data issues in Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central.
hero_sub: Detect data issues, quantify financial impact, and monitor your ERP health continuously - with a first reliable loss baseline in just minutes.
hero_trust_1: Deep scan of your Business Central data
hero_trust_2: Realistic annual loss and savings potential
hero_trust_3: Continuous monitoring for critical data issues
metric_loss_sub: Monetized impact based on your detected data issues.

metric_save_sub: Estimated realistic savings after addressing key issues.
problem_title: Most Business Central systems contain hidden data issues.
problem_sub: Weak data quality often starts as small friction and then accumulates into operational and financial risk across sales, purchasing, finance, and operations.
problem_bullet_1: Missing mandatory fields in master data
problem_bullet_4: Old open documents and blocked ledger entries
problem_card_1_text: Wrong prices and discount mismatches quietly reduce contribution margin.
problem_card_2_text: Teams spend recurring effort on corrections, follow-ups, and data cleanup.
problem_card_3_text: Blocked transactions and unresolved documents slow execution across departments.
problem_card_4_text: Low-quality data distorts KPIs, forecasts, and management decisions.
impact_title: Bad data is not just annoying. It is expensive.
impact_item_1_text: Wrong prices and discounts reduce margin.
impact_item_2_text: Teams spend time fixing avoidable data errors.
impact_item_3_text: Incomplete setups block transactions and approvals.
impact_item_4_text: Low-quality data distorts reports and KPIs.
demo_title: Show management a real business case - not just a data list.
demo_text: BCSentinel connects technical findings with financial relevance so weak data quality becomes visible in euros, priorities, and measurable improvement potential.
demo_list_1: Clear Data Health Score for a fast executive overview
demo_list_2: Estimated annual loss and realistic savings potential
demo_list_3: Issue categories and high-risk areas at a glance
demo_list_4: Strong basis for recurring monitoring and better decisions
solution_title: BCSentinel turns poor data into measurable clarity.
solution_sub: Run a full scan of your Business Central system and instantly see where your data quality is weak, how big the business impact is, and which areas deserve attention first.
solution_card_1_text: Get an immediate management-ready overview of the quality of your ERP data.
solution_card_2_text: Translate data quality problems into realistic economic relevance.
solution_card_3_text: See which data areas and processes are most affected by weak quality.
solution_card_4_text: Build a strong basis for recurring scans and continuous data health monitoring.
how_title: From first assessment to ongoing monitoring.
how_sub: A clear customer path: assess, clean up, validate, and monitor continuously when needed.
step_1_text: Run one deep scan with one scan credit and seven days of dashboard, issue, and report access.
step_2_text: Use findings, executive report, and recommendations to fix the most relevant Business Central data issues.
step_3_text: Get a score, business impact, and issue overview - then unlock actions with Monitoring.
value_title: See what poor data costs - and what fixing it can save.
value_text: Turn weak data quality into a financial conversation that supports prioritization, budget decisions, and internal buy-in.
value_proof: Example: 18 pricing issues - 12 months - 15 minutes rework = EUR 2,835 annual avoidable cost.
pricing_title: Clear product prices for scan and monitoring.
pricing_sub: Choose a one-time assessment start, a cleanup validation, or continuous monitoring.
product_monitoring_annual_title: Annual monitoring with fixed yearly price.

plan_assessment_fit: Best for teams that want to quantify risk before changing processes.
plan_monitoring_5: Clear purchase path from insight to execution
plan_monitoring_fit: Best for teams that want fast execution and measurable savings.
plan_guarantee_3: Move from Assessment or Validation Check when needed
security_sub: BCSentinel is designed to support privacy-first SaaS usage and to create trust in sensitive ERP contexts.
partners_title: Build recurring revenue as a BCSentinel partner.
partners_sub: Recommend BCSentinel to Business-Central customers and earn 30% commission on qualified Monitoring revenue.
partners_value_3: Clear payout overview with status transparency
partners_value_4: Sales-ready material for demo and onboarding
partners_flow_1: Get your partner code and referral link
partners_flow_2: Track referred tenants and their status in your portal
partners_flow_3: See commissions move from pending to approved and paid
partners_note: Portal includes account details, referred customers, commission status, and payout history.
security_card_text: Hosted in Germany, designed for GDPR-conscious businesses, and built with a focus on secure processing, tenant separation, and minimal exposure of sensitive ERP information.
faq_1_a: BCSentinel scans your Business Central data quality across key areas such as customers, vendors, items, finance, purchasing, sales, and process-related records.
faq_2_a: BCSentinel transfers tenant and company identification, metadata, configuration data, scan results, findings, and aggregated quality metrics needed for analysis, dashboard, reports, and license or credit checks.
faq_3_q: Are API tokens used in reports or URLs?
faq_3_a: No. API tokens should not appear in reports or URLs. Dashboard embeds use short-lived embed tokens, and report share links have expiry times.
faq_4_a: A scan credit allows one paid deep scan. Assessment and Validation Check each include one scan credit.
faq_5_q: What is the difference between Assessment and Validation Check?
faq_5_a: Assessment is the first deep scan. Validation Check is the follow-up scan after cleanup to confirm progress and refresh the report view.
faq_6_a: Monitoring makes sense when data quality should remain visible after the first assessment or after cleanup work.
faq_7_q: How long do I have access to dashboard, issues, and reports?
faq_7_a: Assessment and Validation Check provide seven days of access. Monitoring keeps access active while the subscription is active.
faq_8_q: Does BCSentinel work with Business Central Cloud?
faq_8_a: Yes. BCSentinel is prepared for Business Central Cloud and does not require direct database access.
faq_9_q: Is BCSentinel already live in Microsoft AppSource?
faq_9_a: No. AppSource readiness is in preparation; the current flow is for pilot and direct customer onboarding.
cta_title: See what your Business Central data is costing you before it gets more expensive.
cta_sub: Start with Assessment, validate improvements with a follow-up scan, and use Monitoring when data quality should stay visible.
cta_note: Start with Assessment, validate improvements, and move to Monitoring when continuous control is needed.
value_stat_1_sub: Estimated financial impact caused by detected data quality issues.
value_stat_1_link: How is this calculated? See 5 examples
value_stat_2_sub: Realistic saving potential after resolving the most important issues first.
value_stat_3_sub: Each issue uses affected records, probability, effort, hourly rate, and yearly frequency.

box_text: The process was cancelled or could not be completed. You can try again at any time.
hint: Note: Existing data remains available. You can continue later.
fineprint: Status changes can take a few seconds. Refresh the page again if needed.
fuerm_note: Please complete all required fields. After successful submission, you will receive a short confirmation.

privacy_text: I agree that my information may be processed to handle my request. More information is available in the privacy policy.

editor_title: Responsible for content according to Section 18 para. 2 MStV
eu_text: The European Commission provides a platform for online dispute resolution:
eu_text_2: You can find our email address above in this legal notice.
consumer_title: Consumer dispute resolution / universal arbitration board
pruefessional_text: If your activity requires specific professional regulations, authorizations or supervisory authorities, add the required information in this section.
tips_1: If you operate as a sole proprietor, use your civil name and, if applicable, your business designation.
tips_2: If you use a Ltd., GmbH or UG, the company name, legal form and legal representation must be stated correctly.
tips_3: If you do not yet have a VAT ID, publish that section only once the final number is available.
tips_4: The arbitration wording should match your actual business practice.
step_4_text: Move to monthly or annual monitoring when data quality should remain visible over time.
fuermula_title: Estimated Loss follows one transparent model.
fuermula_sub: BCSentinel estimates what weak data does to daily business processes: how many records are affected, how often an issue matters, and how much internal effort it creates.
fuermula_box: Estimated Loss = Affected Records - Probability - Minutes - Hour Rate - Frequency
fuermula_l1_text: How many records are actually impacted by the issue.
fuermula_l2_text: How often the issue creates a real business effect.
fuermula_l4_text: Internal blended hourly rate used for the business case.
fuermula_l5_text: How often the same effect occurs during the year.
fuermula_l6_text: Not every faulty record hurts every time. That keeps the estimate realistic.
fuermula_note: The examples use a realistic internal hourly rate. Values are intentionally conservative.
top5_title: Five concrete examples customers can immediately understand.
top5_sub: These examples show where time is lost, processes get blocked, and money leaks out unnoticed.
all_title: The remaining checks in short form.
all_sub: Individual issues may look small, but in live systems they add up to rework, delays, and process friction.
tenant_logic_note: Tenants appear once they are attributed to your partner code.
pruefile_note: Profile changes apply to the partner account.
consent: I agree that my data is processed for partner onboarding and contact purposes.
login_link: Already a partner? Go to login.
profile_note: Profile changes apply to the partner account.
professional_text: If your activity requires specific professional regulations, authorizations or supervisory authorities, add the required information in this section.
summary_title: What poor data costs and why the number is credible.
summary_sub: The loss estimate is based on recurring operational effort, delays and avoidable process friction.
docs_getting_started_title: From first setup to ongoing monitoring.
docs_getting_started_lead: The typical flow leads from tenant registration through Assessment and Deep Scan to cleanup, Validation Check and Monitoring.
docs_flow_1_text: Connect the Business Central tenant and confirm data processing consent.
docs_flow_2_text: Purchase an Assessment or receive scan credit during pilot onboarding.
docs_flow_3_text: Enter API URL and token and run the connection test in Business Central.
docs_flow_4_text: Run the scan and review status and history.
docs_flow_5_text: Use Dashboard, findings, issue details and Executive Report.
docs_flow_6_text: Clean up data, run Validation Check and activate Monitoring when needed.
docs_setup_lead: Setup stays intentionally lightweight and understandable for BC admins.
docs_setup_1_text: BCSentinel is configured directly inside Business Central.
docs_setup_2_text: Refresh license and scan credits before starting a Deep Scan.
docs_setup_3_text: BCSentinel uses the customer language from Business Central where available.
docs_screenshots_lead: These areas are prepared for final product and AppSource screenshots.
docs_screen_1_text: Screenshot area for setup and registration.
docs_screen_2_text: Screenshot area for scan runs and status.
docs_screen_3_text: Screenshot area for dashboard and KPIs.
docs_screen_4_text: Screenshot area for findings and recommendations.
docs_screen_6_text: Optional screenshot area for admin and pricing.
docs_videos_lead: Short videos can explain onboarding and typical workflows.
docs_video_1_text: Video area for first setup and registration.
docs_video_2_text: Video area for scan start and history.
docs_video_3_text: Video area for Executive Report and sharing.
docs_troubleshooting_lead: Common startup issues usually relate to license, registration, scan status or link expiry.
docs_trouble_1_text: Refresh license or purchase Assessment / Validation Check.
docs_trouble_2_text: Run registration again and verify API URL and token.
docs_trouble_3_text: Check link expiry and public base URL configuration.
docs_trouble_4_text: Wait for scan completion and refresh dashboard data.
docs_trouble_5_text: Refresh license so tenant language can be updated.
docs_faq_section_lead: This product documentation will continue to be expanded.
docs_faq_1_text: This is product documentation and will continue to be expanded.
docs_faq_2_text: The scan reads metadata and results; cleanup actions remain controlled by the customer.
docs_faq_3_text: Use the contact page for support, demo and onboarding requests.
terms_usage_lead: BCSentinel combines SaaS, backend and Business Central extension.
terms_usage_1_text: BCSentinel combines a hosted service with a Business Central extension.
terms_usage_2_text: Analysis results support decisions; they are not legal, tax or financial advice.
terms_usage_3_text: Customers remain responsible for reviewing data and implementing changes.
terms_licenses_lead: Products are Assessment, Validation Check and monthly or annual Monitoring.
terms_license_1_text: One initial Deep Scan and limited access window.
terms_license_3_text: Monthly or annual access for ongoing visibility.
terms_payments_lead: Payment and subscription flows depend on product and provider.
terms_payment_1_text: Payments may be processed through the configured billing provider.
terms_payment_2_text: Monitoring may renew monthly or annually depending on product selection.
terms_payment_3_text: Cancellation and termination are handled according to contract and billing setup.
terms_customer_data_lead: Data handling is described in the privacy information.
terms_data_1_text: Data processing is described in the privacy information.
terms_data_2_text: The BC admin confirms data processing consent before scans.
terms_data_3_text: Reports are generated from available data and do not guarantee business outcomes.
terms_liability_lead: Final legal review required before production/AppSource publication.
terms_liability_1_text: Final legal review required before production/AppSource publication.
terms_liability_2_text: Liability limits must be finalized for the actual operating entity.
terms_liability_3_text: This page does not claim external certification or legal approval.
terms_termination_lead: Access can end when licenses expire or subscriptions are cancelled.
terms_termination_1_text: Access can end when licenses expire or subscriptions are cancelled.
terms_termination_2_text: Retention and deletion follow privacy and operational policies.
terms_termination_3_text: Contact BCSentinel for account or access questions.
security_privacy_principles_lead: BCSentinel processes only needed data and relies on transparent consent.
security_principle_1_text: Only data needed for scans, dashboards, reports and licensing is processed.
security_principle_2_text: Data Processing Consent is required before registration and scans.
security_principle_3_text: Business Central Cloud does not require direct database access.
security_tokens_lead: Tokens are purpose-bound and short-lived where possible.
security_token_1_text: API tokens are stored in Business Central IsolatedStorage.
security_token_2_text: Analytics embeds use short-lived purpose-bound tokens.
security_token_3_text: Report share links are time-limited and separate from API tokens.
security_tenant_lead: Requests and data are scoped by tenant.
security_tenant_1_text: Requests and data are scoped to the authenticated tenant.
security_tenant_2_text: Admin and partner access use separated authentication flows.
security_tenant_3_text: Production deployment should use HTTPS/TLS behind the reverse proxy.
security_audit_lead: Admin changes and operations status should remain traceable.
security_audit_1_text: Admin changes are logged where audit logging is available.
security_audit_2_text: Health and readiness endpoints support operations checks.
security_audit_3_text: Production operations should follow the documented backup and restore runbook.
security_disclosure_lead: Security findings can be reported through the contact channel.
security_disclosure_1_text: Security findings can be reported through the contact channel.
security_disclosure_2_text: This page avoids claims of certification unless formally achieved.
security_disclosure_3_text: Security documentation will evolve with production readiness.
